famous quote:
"It may be hard for an egg to turn into a bird: it would be a jolly sight harder for a bird to learn to fly while remaining an egg. We are like eggs at present. And you cannot go on indefinitely being just an ordinary, decent egg. We must be hatched or go bad."
-- C.S. Lewis (Mere Christianity)
Interview Text:
Dena:

(part 3)

Dena has had a long history serving God so I asked her, How do you hear from God? 
Here's what she said: 

"Sometimes I hear God through other people. For example, in the story of my calling, when my kids were tiny, I was vacation bible school coordinator. 
I led and developed some creative curriculums. And people said, you need to publish this. I felt like Moses, "Who am I to write a curriculum?" That was the beginning of my calling. It was made up of little nudges.

Then she told me a story where God led her to her path through difficult situations.
Here's what she said:

"I taught or led all sorts of church programs for many years and developed and learned a lot of ways to run the programs. But I was getting burned out. Then our church hired a girl who wanted to do everything differently, and she threw out all my work. I started putting my resume out for a position somewhere. There were 9 months that I felt lost. 

"I asked, 'God, you called me to do this, why are you stopping it?' I felt like Jonah in the whale, but when was it going to end? 

"About that time I started seeing rainbows all the time. Every time I saw one I felt like I understood he was saying "it's gonna be okay." Eventually I got a job at a church around the block. I felt God was finally spitting me out of the whale onto the shore. I developed a lot of creative methods and curriculum and brought it back to my home church [when she went back to it]."
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
